commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a compact live status dashboard glance to the tray flyout, including a render-free summary builder, snapshot fields, a Diagnostics menu action, and focused tray unit tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new tray UX feature PR rather than a reported current-main bug. Source inspection confirms current main lacks the requested compact tray glance.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This draft feature PR needs maintainer UX/product review and contributor-provided visible proof; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: Add tray live dashboard glance
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the tray flyout exposed actions and rich sub-flyouts, but the top-level surface did not provide a compact live status summary.
- Why it matters: operators should be able to see gateway health, freshness, session activity, node/session counts, and usage at a glance before choosing a deeper action.
- What changed: added a render-free `TrayDashboardSummaryBuilder`, a dashboard glance card in the tray menu, snapshot fields for heartbeat/preview data, a Diagnostics action, and unit coverage for summary behavior.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway protocol changes, no capability permission changes, no tray right-click behavior changes, and no MCP server behavior changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs / instructions
- [x] Tests / validation
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Tray / WinUI UX
- [ ] Windows node capability
- [ ] Local MCP / `winnode`
- [ ] Gateway / connection / pairing
- [ ] Setup / onboarding
- [ ] Permissions / privacy / security
- [x] Tests / CI / docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] Related to a bug or regression

## Validation

- `./build.ps1` — passed; built Shared, Cli, WinNodeCli, SetupEngine, WinUI.
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj --no-restore` — passed: 2513 passed, 31 skipped, 2544 total.
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj --no-restore --filter "FullyQualifiedName~TrayDashboardSummaryBuilderTests"` — passed: 48 passed, 0 skipped, 48 total.
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj --no-restore` — passed: 1263 passed, 0 skipped, 1263 total.

## Real behavior proof

- Environment tested: Windows ARM64 worktree `bkudiess-tray-live-dashboard`, direct unpackaged WinUI app, isolated data dir under session artifacts, `OPENCLAW_TRAY_PREVIEW_DATA=1` for deterministic sessions/usage data.
- Exact steps or command run:
  - `./run-app-local.ps1 -NoBuild -AllowNonMain` with `OPENCLAW_TRAY_DATA_DIR` set to a throwaway proof directory and `OPENCLAW_TRAY_PREVIEW_DATA=1`.
  - Opened the app with a proof settings file (`EnableMcpServer=true`) to avoid first-run setup prompts.
  - Captured app proof screenshot locally and copied isolated app diagnostics logs.
- Evidence after fix:
  - Safe diagnostic log line from isolated proof run: `{"event":"app.start","metadata":{"nodeMode":false,"useSshTunnel":false}}`.
  - Local artifacts (not uploaded to avoid exposing unrelated desktop content): `C:\Users\bakudies\.copilot\session-state\1321cfd9-b329-4ec7-99e5-941f21233c06\files\pr-proof\live-proof-current-screen.png`, `...\pr-proof\logs\diagnostics.jsonl`.
  - Focused dashboard summary tests validate the actual health/heartbeat/metrics/session-preview summary behavior.
- Observed result: the branch app launches successfully in an isolated profile and validation proves the tray dashboard summary output, action routing, usage/session fallback logic, and preview correlation behavior.
- Not verified / blocked: attaching a tray-flyout screenshot to the PR. This desktop had multiple OpenClaw tray instances registered; taskbar clicks focused Hub windows and the `openclaw://tray-menu` route was bound to an existing instance. I captured local proof artifacts instead and did not upload screenshots containing unrelated desktop content.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No bot review conversations exist yet; this is a draft PR.
